proof_boundary: Signed J-fraction theorem target specification only; not a proof of the all-order determinant signature, the all-order column recurrence, Schur positivity, Jensen-window PF-infinity, RH, or Lambda <= 0.

objects.Delta_r^*: determinant formed by shifting only the final Hankel column to mu_{i+r}

hypotheses: For every degree d>=2 and shift n>=0, H(t) is the actual normalized zeta heat-flow Jensen-window polynomial at lambda=0.
The reciprocal coefficients mu_m=[t^m]1/H(-t) have nonzero Hankel determinants Delta_r for all r needed by the J-fraction.
The signed Hankel signature (-1)^(r(r-1)/2) Delta_r > 0 holds for every r>=1, or an equivalent signed J-fraction parameter statement kappa_n=-lambda_n>0 holds for every n>=1.

The ordinary diagonal Jacobi beta parameters, defined by the last-column shifted determinant formula beta_n=Q_{n+1}-Q_n, have an all-order sign pattern compatible with the finite beta scout, or the proof explains why that finite beta pattern is not part of the required mechanism.
Additional Xi/Phi-specific analytic hypotheses are stated explicitly and do not assume Jensen-window PF-infinity, Jensen hyperbolicity, Laguerre-Polya membership, RH, or Lambda <= 0.
desired_conclusion: mu_m >= 0 for every m>=0, degree d, and shift n.
Equivalently, the normalized column recurrence coefficients are nonnegative for every size.
This proves only the column-shape recurrence subtarget unless separately lifted to all Schur/Toeplitz shapes.
acceptance_conditions: An exact theorem statement with a proof or a cited theorem whose hypotheses are verified for the actual zeta windows.
All-degree and all-shift coverage, not only the finite d=2..8, n=0..20 grid.
A mechanism converting signed Hankel/J-fraction data to coefficientwise nonnegativity of E(t), not only to existence of a formal fraction.
No finite-prefix, endpoint real-rootedness, RH, Laguerre-Polya, or Jensen-hyperbolicity assumption as input.
A clear statement whether the theorem proves only column shapes or also supplies an all-Schur/Toeplitz lift.

fit_rows.theorem_family: ordinary positive Stieltjes/Jacobi S-fraction or J-fraction
fit_rows.fit: Would be a clean coefficient-positivity mechanism if all ordinary fraction parameters were nonnegative.
fit_rows.gap_or_rejection: The exact first signs are a_2=-g_2/g_1 and lambda_1=-g_2, so the standard positive theorem has the wrong sign for positive g_1,g_2.
fit_rows.next_action: Keep this as a rejection gate; do not cite positive Stieltjes/Jacobi fraction machinery for E(t).

fit_rows.theorem_family: signed J-fraction or oscillatory continued-fraction theorem
fit_rows.fit: The finite data exactly supports the signed signature (-1)^(r(r-1)/2) Delta_r>0, kappa_n=-lambda_n>0, and the sharper beta_0>0, beta_1<0, beta_n>0 diagonal pattern.
fit_rows.gap_or_rejection: No theorem is currently identified that turns this signed signature into mu_m>=0 for every actual zeta window.
fit_rows.next_action: Search for an oscillatory, sign-regular, or signed moment theorem matching this signature and the reciprocal finite-support setting.

fit_rows.theorem_family: oscillatory or sign-regular Jacobi matrix after diagonal sign conjugation
fit_rows.fit: The negative ordinary lambda_n, positive kappa_n, and beta_0>0, beta_1<0, beta_n>0 diagonal pattern suggest a tightly constrained sign-conjugated tridiagonal or oscillatory matrix model.
fit_rows.gap_or_rejection: The oscillatory/resolvent fit matrix rejects the standard shortcuts: entrywise nonnegative Jacobi powers fail by lambda_n<0 and beta_1<0, diagonal similarity preserves the closed-path signs, absolute-value majorants overshoot mu_2, classical oscillatory spectral conclusions lack a positive scalar readout, and the positive spectral moment obstruction rejects ordinary positive moment measures by Delta_2=-g_2.
fit_rows.next_action: Use the positive-readout theorem target to search only for a noncircular positive spectral transform or an Xi/Phi-specific positive resolvent kernel whose scalar readout is exactly E(t) and proves mu_m>=0.

fit_rows.theorem_family: production matrix, Riordan, or lattice-path total-positivity model
fit_rows.fit: A modified path model could turn the signed kappa_n into manifestly nonnegative weights after the correct transformation.
fit_rows.gap_or_rejection: The raw ordinary Motzkin/J-fraction path model is rejected as manifest positivity by the mu_2 cancellation and beta_1 diagonal obstruction scout, global length-parity sign repair is rejected by same-length mixed-sign path witnesses, and the absolute-value sign-state cover is rejected at mu_2 by the gap 2*kappa_1>0; the modified signed-model target leaves only genuinely modified state-space, modified production/Riordan/network, oscillatory resolvent, and Xi/Phi positive-cancellation routes live conditionally.
fit_rows.next_action: Use the finite signed-kappa, beta, Motzkin-obstruction, parity-lift obstruction, sign-lift obstruction, and modified signed-model target to fit a genuinely modified production matrix or state-space model, then reject it unless it proves all-order coefficient positivity.

fit_rows.theorem_family: indefinite moment or Hankel determinant solvability
fit_rows.fit: Indefinite moment language can describe the alternating determinant signature.
fit_rows.gap_or_rejection: Solvability or representation of an indefinite moment problem does not by itself imply coefficientwise nonnegativity of E(t), Jensen-window PF, or Laguerre-Polya membership.
fit_rows.next_action: Use only as interpretation unless paired with an explicit positivity theorem.

fit_rows.fit: The finite grid is strong falsification pressure and gives the exact signs a theorem should explain.
fit_rows.gap_or_rejection: Finite signature rows cannot be extrapolated to all degrees, shifts, or determinant orders.
fit_rows.next_action: Use finite evidence to test theorem candidates, never as the theorem.

fit_rows.fit: If H(t) already has nonpositive real roots, the reciprocal has a positive product model and the signed Hankel signature is compatible.
fit_rows.gap_or_rejection: Using this as an input assumes the Jensen-window endpoint whose proof is the target.
fit_rows.next_action: Use endpoint real-rootedness only as a consistency check for finite Sturm/PF windows.

source_anchors.use: finite signed Jacobi diagonal beta-parameter evidence
finite obstruction to raw ordinary Motzkin/J-fraction manifest positivity
finite obstruction to global length-parity sign repairs of the raw Motzkin model
finite derived obstruction to absolute-value sign-state covers of the raw Motzkin model
classification of dead raw sign repairs and live modified signed-model candidates
fit/misfit matrix for oscillatory, sign-regular, and resolvent theorem shortcuts
positive scalar readout theorem target for the two surviving oscillatory/resolvent subroutes
ordinary positive moment-measure obstruction for the spectral-transform subroute
theorem-ecosystem anchor for Hankel, continued fractions, total positivity, and root localization

kill_gates: Reject any proof that uses only the finite d=2..8, n=0..20 signed J-fraction or signed Jacobi beta scout.
Reject any proof that uses the raw ordinary Motzkin/J-fraction path expansion as a manifestly nonnegative production matrix.
Reject any proof that repairs the raw ordinary Motzkin/J-fraction path expansion only by a global length-parity sign or diagonal sign conjugation.
Reject any proof that cites ordinary positive Stieltjes/Jacobi fractions for E(t) despite the negative first parameter.
Reject any proof that assumes endpoint real-rootedness, Jensen hyperbolicity, Laguerre-Polya membership, RH, or Lambda <= 0.
Reject any proof that proves only formal existence of a signed fraction without coefficientwise nonnegativity of E(t).
Reject any proof that claims to close jwpf_06 from column recurrence positivity without an all-Schur/Toeplitz lift.

summary.main_finding: The signed J-fraction route now has a precise missing theorem: convert the all-order signed reciprocal-Hankel/Jacobi signature, now sharpened by the signed Jacobi beta pattern, raw Motzkin path obstruction, global parity-lift obstruction, state-space sign-lift obstruction, modified signed-model target, oscillatory/resolvent fit matrix, positive-readout theorem target, and positive spectral moment obstruction, into coefficientwise nonnegativity of E(t)=1/H(-t) for every actual zeta Jensen window, without using endpoint real-rootedness or finite extrapolation.

invariants: The standard positive Stieltjes/Jacobi fraction row remains rejected.
The signed J-fraction row remains open_missing_theorem.
Finite evidence rows cannot close the target.
The theorem target proves at most the column recurrence unless an all-Schur/Toeplitz lift is supplied.
